mechanism: System assigns categorical codes to spatial responses based on stimuli presented across multiple trial blocks, compares distributions of category selections (northern vs. southern, eastern vs. western) across participant populations and task conditions using cross-tabulation and interaction analysis, evaluates whether assignment of stimulus category affects subsequent choice via statistical testing.

Text documents are tokenized by reporter identity, references to categorical identifiers (nationality) are counted, time periods are partitioned by event boundary, counts aggregate within period and reporter groups, statistical test compares frequency distributions across periods.
distribution: categorical reference counts across time periods

mechanism: Organism presses lever under delay-exposure or immediacy-exposure training protocols; preference is assessed via binary choice trials at increasing delays, trial-by-trial choices are aggregated, area-under-curve (AUC) computed from percent choice at each delay, between-group comparison via linear mixed-effects model on logistic binomial choice data.

Participant holds dynamometer under conjugate schedule: image transparency decreases per second at 10% per second unless force applied, image clarity restored proportionally to force (10% clarity per 1 kg); mean and peak force recorded per trial, force ranks compared to preference ranks via chi-square test of independence and post-hoc goodness-of-fit tests.
distribution: continuous force values ranked into ordinal outcomes

mechanism: Latent variable model (structural equation model) specifies five interconnected subscales as first-order factors; observed item responses (4-point Likert agreement scale) serve as measured indicators of each latent construct; maximum likelihood estimation with Satorra-Bentler correction produces standardized path coefficients; nested chi-square tests compare competing models.
distribution: ordinal Likert responses mapped to latent factors

mechanism: Personality dimensions (five factors) measured as continuous scores; body weight measured in three anthropometric forms; ordinary least squares (OLS) regression and generalized linear models with logit link regress outcome measures on predictor trait scores, covariates, and interaction terms; stratified analysis by gender tests moderation effects.
distribution: continuous weight outcomes, continuous personality predictors
